key_highlights: SpaceX's $60 billion acquisition of AI coding startup Cursor and its stock surge signal a major tech M&A and AI infrastructure theme.
US-Iran peace deal drives oil prices to three-month lows, with potential to ease inflation and reshape Middle East oil trade.
Kevin Warsh's first Fed meeting as chair is the week's pivotal event, with markets expecting a hawkish stance on Trumpflation.
Robinhood's 10% staff cut reflects ongoing fintech cost-cutting, while Fox's $22 billion Roku acquisition reshapes streaming.
Bitcoin and gold are firming ahead of the Fed, with yen shorts at nine-year highs as BOJ decision looms.
India's exports hit six-month high on weaker rupee, and US imports surge 54%.
Elon Musk's trillionaire status highlights growing wealth inequality, while ultra-rich Americans seek exit strategies.
D-Wave and Rigetti quantum computing stocks gain attention on roadmap updates and long-term potential.
HELOC and home equity loan rates remain attractive amid the lock-in effect, with money market rates up to 4.1% APY.
Cantor Fitzgerald raises Sandisk price target, and BofA raises Arista Networks target, indicating bullishness on data storage and networking.

sectors.trend: Massive M&A and capex push: SpaceX acquires Cursor for $60B, Fox buys Roku for $22B, and AI data center stocks surge on AMD partnership. AI wars intensify among Microsoft, Google, Amazon.
sectors.implications: Investors should overweight AI infrastructure plays (data centers, networking, storage) as hyperscalers compete for power and talent. Cursor acquisition validates AI coding tools as a high-growth niche.

sectors.trend: Oil prices collapse to three-month lows on US-Iran peace deal, with contango returning. Middle East oil trade irrevocably changed. Qatar plans rapid LNG restart after Hormuz reopening.
sectors.implications: Short-term deflationary boost for consumers and airlines, but oil price floor is questionable. Energy stocks may face headwinds. Strategic reserve at 43-year low adds long-term supply risk.

sectors.trend: Robinhood cuts 10% staff to become leaner. Goldman sees room for rally beyond AI winners. DE Shaw closes $5B fund. Bondholders face $4B tax threat. EBA eyes bigger role for banks in capital buffers.
sectors.implications: Fintech consolidation continues; cost discipline is key. Broader market rally may rotate into value/cyclicals. Credit markets face volatility from tax disputes and regulatory changes.

sectors.trend: Starbucks Korea closes early for history training after marketing row. Nike can't afford to lose World Cup. Verizon debuts new plan and loyalty program. Home equity loans help navigate lock-in effect.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending resilient but selective. Home equity remains a key financing tool. Brand reputation management critical for global retailers.

sectors.trend: Medicare identity fraud widespread ($8 per stolen identity). Biocon on track to be top insulin maker. Obamacare becomes too costly for many, leaving uninsured.
sectors.implications: Healthcare fraud detection and cybersecurity stocks may benefit. Insulin price competition could pressure pharma margins. Policy risk around healthcare affordability remains.

sectors.trend: D-Wave releases gate-model roadmap, Rigetti and IonQ highlighted as top computer hardware stocks for next 5 years.
sectors.implications: Quantum computing is a high-risk, high-reward long-term play. Near-term revenue still limited, but roadmap milestones drive investor enthusiasm.

sectors.trend: HELOC and home equity loan rates attractive; spread between HELOC and HEL is 61 bps. Beijing's tighter capital controls may dampen Hong Kong's housing rebound.
sectors.implications: Homeowners can tap equity at favorable rates. Hong Kong property market faces headwinds from capital outflow restrictions.

key_themes: AI M&A and infrastructure spending boom
Geopolitical detente driving oil price collapse and inflation relief
Central bank policy divergence (Fed hawkish, BOJ uncertain)
Wealth inequality and capital flight from US
Quantum computing as emerging long-term play
Home equity as a consumer financing tool
India's export growth on weaker rupee
SpaceX's valuation surge and retail frenzy

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios; favor floating-rate instruments and cash. Hedge equity exposure with put spreads or defensive sectors (utilities, healthcare).
Maintain diversified energy exposure with both upstream and midstream. Consider long-dated call options on oil if deal falters. Monitor Hormuz reopening and US reserve levels.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: BOJ Rate Decision / Yen Carry Trade Unwind
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to yen-funded carry trades. Hedge USD/JPY with options. Bitcoin and gold may benefit from yen volatility.
Diversify geographically; overweight US and India. Monitor Starlink regulatory risks in India and China capital controls.
Avoid overexposure to Medicare-dependent firms. Consider cybersecurity and fraud detection stocks as hedges.
Monitor policy proposals on wealth taxes. Ultra-rich capital flight may pressure luxury real estate and certain tax havens.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Credit Market Stress (Indonesia, Bond Tax Feud)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to Indonesian credit. Avoid bonds involved in tax disputes. Favor high-quality investment grade.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy AI infrastructure and data center stocks on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: AI capex cycle is still early; AMD partnership surge and SpaceX's Cursor acquisition confirm long-term demand. Arista Networks (ANET), Dell (DELL), and Sandisk (SNDK) are well-positioned.

opportunities.recommendation: Add to positions in home equity lenders and consumer finance
opportunities.rationale: HELOC and home equity loan rates remain attractive; lock-in effect drives demand. Verizon's new plan and loyalty program indicate competitive consumer finance landscape.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider long positions in gold and bitcoin ahead of Fed and BOJ decisions
opportunities.rationale: Gold firming, bitcoin at two-week highs. Yen shorts at nine-year high create asymmetric upside for safe havens if BOJ surprises or Fed disappoints.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in Indian export-oriented and pharmaceutical companies
opportunities.rationale: Weaker rupee boosts exports to six-month high; Biocon on track to be top insulin maker. US import surge of 54% benefits Indian IT and pharma.
Fox's $22B Roku deal positions it as a streaming powerhouse. Synergies with Fox's content and Roku's platform could drive long-term value.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios; increase cash and money market exposure
defensive_moves.rationale: Fed likely to be hawkish; money market rates up to 4.1% APY offer attractive risk-free returns. Short-term Treasuries preferred.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge oil exposure with put spreads or long-dated calls
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil price floor is questionable despite Iran deal; US reserve at 43-year low adds upside risk. Use options to manage volatility.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Rotate from high-flying tech into value and cyclicals
defensive_moves.rationale: Goldman sees room for rally to broaden beyond AI winners. Fed hawkishness may favor financials, energy, and industrials.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid Indonesian credit and bonds with tax disputes
defensive_moves.rationale: Worst Indonesian credit volatility in years; $4B tax threat in credit feud. Stick to high-quality investment grade.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets are in a wait-and-see mode ahead of Kevin Warsh's first Fed meeting. Oil's collapse provides a deflationary tailwind, but hawkish Fed rhetoric could cap equity gains. Volatility likely to increase around BOJ decision and Fed announcement. Bitcoin and gold may rally if central banks disappoint. Sector rotation from AI winners to value/cyclicals may accelerate.
market_outlook.long_term: Geopolitical detente (US-Iran) and AI infrastructure buildout are positive for global growth and productivity. However, wealth inequality, healthcare costs, and potential trade tensions remain structural risks. India and Southeast Asia benefit from supply chain diversification. Quantum computing and space (SpaceX) are emerging long-term themes. Bond yields may stay elevated if Fed remains hawkish, but inflation relief from lower oil prices could allow for eventual easing.
key_catalysts: Kevin Warsh's first Fed meeting and rate decision (this week)
BOJ rate decision and impact on yen carry trade
US-Iran peace deal signing and oil price trajectory
SpaceX stock performance and potential inclusion in indices
Fox-Roku merger completion and streaming landscape
India export data and rupee movement
Hormuz reopening and global grain/oil flows
US strategic petroleum reserve refill plans
watch_list: Fed funds rate and dot plot projections
BOJ policy rate and yen level
WTI and Brent crude oil prices
SpaceX valuation vs Amazon and Microsoft
Home equity loan rates and HELOC spreads
